titel: Stronger protection for the independence of courts and judges
html:
In a state governed by the rule of law, it is fundamental that every citizen can have their case heard in a fair trial by independent and impartial courts. To strengthen and give lasting protection to the independence of the courts and judges, the government proposes, among other things, the following.

• A new act on courts and judges is introduced.

• The authority for central court administration is given a more independent position in relation to the government and is renamed from the National Courts Administration to the Courts Board.

• A nomination procedure for the Judges Proposals Board is introduced, under which the government is bound by the board's proposals in appointment cases.

• A new board authority, the Judicial Accountability Board, is established.

• A rule on the retirement age for permanent judges is introduced.

• The Chancellor of Justice will no longer supervise the court system.

The new legislation is proposed to enter into force on 1 April 2027.
